label: Device Agent (on-device LLM harness demo)
description: On-device LLM agent harness for React Native, built on llama.rn. This card describes the example demo app's configuration: Gemma 3 270M Instruct (Q8_0 GGUF) running fully offline after a one-time model download, with all 7 built-in tool groups enabled plus one custom tool. There is no network endpoint; this document is descriptive/discovery metadata only, not a live callable A2A agent.

endpoints.note: No live endpoints. This agent runs fully offline, on-device; there is no network invocation surface.

skills.description: Make outbound HTTP requests (http_request) using the device's global fetch.
Read, write, and list files in app-sandboxed storage (read_file, write_file, list_files) via expo-file-system.
Read and write the system clipboard (get_clipboard, set_clipboard) via expo-clipboard.
Schedule local device notifications (schedule_notification) via expo-notifications.
Search the device's contacts (search_contacts) via expo-contacts.
List and create calendar events (list_calendar_events, create_calendar_event) via expo-calendar.
Read the device's current geographic location (get_current_location) via expo-location.
Return the current device date/time (ISO 8601). A custom tool defined by the demo app, not part of the package's built-in tool catalog.

baseline_model.name: Gemma 3 270M Instruct
baseline_model.license: Gemma Terms of Use (restrictive, non-OSI-approved) — https://ai.google.dev/gemma/terms

x_device_agent.signature_status: unsigned — this document is descriptive metadata only and carries no cryptographic signature.
schema_deviations: Does not include capabilities.authentication, which the underlying lean AgentFacts schema (agentfacts.org/schema/v1) technically requires. Omitted deliberately: OS-level runtime permissions (see device_permissions) don't fit an OAuth/API-key-shaped auth model, and bending requiredScopes to hold Android permission strings would mislead generic A2A/OASF tooling into thinking this exposes a real credentialed auth flow.
known_limitations: No network invocation endpoint — this document is descriptive/discovery-only, not a live callable A2A agent. endpoints.static is intentionally empty.
Not registered with the live NANDA index (index.projectnanda.org) — resolvable only via this repo's facts_url, and only by someone who already has it.
No cryptographic signature — authenticity and integrity of this document cannot be verified; treat it as self-published metadata.
No DID or resolvable URN — 'id' is a plain string tied to the npm package name, not a decentralized identifier.
Deliberately omits capabilities.authentication (see schema_deviations above) in favor of the device_permissions block.
Model license (Gemma Terms of Use) is restrictive and non-OSI-approved; redistribution and use-case restrictions apply beyond what's summarized here — see the linked terms for the authoritative text.
No GGUF checksum or model-file integrity verification is declared.
No latency, throughput, or maxTokens figures are declared for any skill — none have been measured.
No performance, reputation, or certification data — no telemetry pipeline or third-party audit exists for this project.
Gemma 3 270M is a very small model; tool-call reliability is expected to be weaker than with larger instruction-tuned models. This has not been formally benchmarked.
No automated or manual end-to-end test evidence backs this card yet.
Describes the example demo app's configuration only (all 7 tool groups enabled); the underlying react-native-device-agent package lets any consuming app enable a different subset.
If/when a real backend (e.g. the Host39 plan in .vscode/host39_plan.txt) is built, this card's id, endpoints, and signature fields will need real replacement, not incremental filling-in.
